questions.questionText:
The frequency table shows the number of hours students spent studying last week.

| Hours | Frequency |
|-------|-----------|
| 1     | 4         |
| 2     | 6         |
| 3     | 5         |
| 4     | 3         |
| 5     | 2         |

Calculate the mean number of hours studied.
questions.explanation: Mean from frequency table: ОЈfx Г· ОЈf.

questions.context:
The grouped frequency table shows the masses (in kg) of 40 parcels.

| Mass (kg) | Frequency |
|-----------|----------|
| 0 < m в‰¤ 2 | 6        |
| 2 < m в‰¤ 4 | 14       |
| 4 < m в‰¤ 6 | 12       |
| 6 < m в‰¤ 8 | 5        |
| 8 < m в‰¤ 10| 3        |

parts.label: a
workingSteps: Highest frequency = 14, in class 2 < m в‰¤ 4

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Calculate an estimate of the mean mass.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText:
Show that the class containing the median is 2 < m в‰¤ 4.

**Note**: Use the cumulative frequency approach to identify which class the 20th and 21st values fall in.

markScheme.desc: Median between 20th and 21st values (40 values total)
Cumulative: в‰¤2: 6, в‰¤4: 20; 20th value is the last in 2<mв‰¤4; 21st value is first in 4<mв‰¤6; median lies in 2<mв‰¤4 using convention that 20th is within this class вњ“

workingSteps: 20th value falls at the upper boundary of 2<mв‰¤4 (cumulative = 20)
The median = (20th+21st)/2 so the median class is given as 2<mв‰¤4 since the 20th value is in that class

parts.label: d
parts.questionText:
A parcel is chosen at random.
Find the probability that its mass is more than 6 kg.

parts.label: e
parts.questionText: Explain why the mean calculated in part (b) is only an estimate.
parts.correctAnswer: The exact values within each class are unknown; midpoints are used as approximations

markScheme.desc: Individual values not known; midpoints are used as an approximation oe

questions.questionText:
The mean of six values is 14. The values are:
$$x, \ x+2, \ x+5, \ 18, \ 21, \ 23$$

(a) Show that $3x + 69 = 84$.
(b) Hence find the value of $x$.
(c) Find the median of the six values when $x = 5$.
questions.explanation: Use mean Г— n to find total sum, then form and solve an equation.
